narratives.name: AI Infrastructure Buildout
narratives.thesis: Demand for AI-specific chips and related infrastructure continues to drive strong performance for semiconductor companies, with significant investments from hyperscalers and enterprise.
watchFor: Hyperscaler capex cuts at next earnings
NVDA revenue growth slows below 10%
New competitive threats emerge from Huawei or other players

narratives.name: GLP-1 Obesity Wave
narratives.thesis: The development and market adoption of GLP-1 obesity drugs continue to be a major growth driver for pharmaceutical companies, with strong clinical trial results fueling investor optimism.
watchFor: New competitors enter the GLP-1 market with superior efficacy or safety
Unexpected side effects emerge from long-term GLP-1 use
PBMs or insurers impose significant restrictions on GLP-1 coverage

narratives.name: AI Data Center Infrastructure
narratives.thesis: Companies providing hardware for AI data centers, including servers and networking equipment, are seeing increased demand and strong order backlogs.
watchFor: Major data center operators signal slowing expansion plans
Increased competition from white-box server manufacturers
Supply chain disruptions for critical components

narratives.name: AI Data Center Power Demand
narratives.thesis: The surging demand for power from AI data centers is creating significant opportunities for independent power producers, leading to long-term power deals and accelerated growth.
watchFor: Hyperscalers reduce or delay data center expansion plans
Regulatory hurdles or delays for new power generation projects
Significant increase in natural gas prices, impacting power generation costs

narratives.name: AI Software & Cloud Adoption
narratives.thesis: Enterprises are increasingly adopting AI-powered software and cloud solutions, driving growth for companies offering these services and platforms.
watchFor: Enterprise IT spending slowdown
Increased regulatory scrutiny on AI data usage
Major cloud providers report decelerating AI revenue growth

narratives.name: Aerospace & Defense Spending
narratives.thesis: Increased geopolitical tensions and strong government spending are driving robust demand and order backlogs for aerospace and defense contractors.
watchFor: Major defense contract cancellations or delays
De-escalation of geopolitical conflicts
Government budget cuts impacting defense spending

narratives.name: AI Security Demand
narratives.thesis: The proliferation of AI and cloud adoption is driving increased demand for cybersecurity solutions, particularly those integrating AI for threat detection and response.
watchFor: Major cybersecurity breaches impacting leading vendors
Increased competition leading to pricing pressure
Enterprise budget cuts impacting security spending

narratives.name: Digital Ad & Content Dominance
narratives.thesis: Major internet content and information platforms continue to dominate the digital advertising landscape, leveraging AI for improved targeting and content delivery.
watchFor: Significant slowdown in digital ad spending growth
Increased regulatory pressure on data privacy and ad targeting
New competitive threats from emerging platforms

narratives.name: Semiconductor Equipment & Materials AI Integration
narratives.thesis: Companies providing equipment and materials for semiconductor manufacturing are benefiting from the need for advanced packaging and increased fabrication capacity driven by AI chip demand.
watchFor: TSMC or Intel cut capex guidance
New export controls on advanced manufacturing equipment
Significant slowdown in global chip demand

narratives.name: Restaurants Consumer Spending Headwinds
narratives.thesis: The restaurant industry is experiencing headwinds as consumers tighten discretionary spending due to inflationary pressures, leading to cautious outlooks from major players.
watchFor: Starbucks reports further decline in comparable store sales
Consumer discretionary spending data shows continued weakness
Major restaurant chains issue profit warnings

narratives.name: Credit Services Strength
narratives.thesis: Credit services companies are demonstrating strength through strategic initiatives and positive analyst coverage, despite broader market rebalancing by major investors.
watchFor: Consumer credit defaults rise sharply
Regulatory caps on interchange fees or lending rates
Significant slowdown in consumer spending

narratives.name: Residential Construction Strength
narratives.thesis: The residential construction sector continues to show resilience, potentially driven by persistent housing demand and limited supply, despite broader economic concerns.
watchFor: Mortgage rates spike above 7.5%
Housing starts data shows significant decline
Lennar reports weaker-than-expected order growth

narratives.name: Healthcare Plans Stability
narratives.thesis: Healthcare plans are demonstrating stability and strategic positioning, with some companies highlighted for strong dividend growth and AI integration.
watchFor: Unexpected increases in medical costs or claims
New government regulations impacting insurance profitability
Significant competitive pressure on premium pricing

narratives.name: Integrated Freight & Logistics Strategic Growth
narratives.thesis: Logistics companies are pursuing strategic acquisitions and benefiting from tariff refunds, positioning them for potential growth despite broader economic uncertainties.
watchFor: Acquisition deals fall through or face regulatory hurdles
Global trade volumes decline significantly
Fuel prices spike, increasing operational costs

narratives.name: Asset Management AI Adoption
narratives.thesis: Asset management firms are increasingly adopting AI technologies for investment strategies and operational efficiency, attracting strategic partnerships and capital inflows.
watchFor: AI investment strategies fail to outperform benchmarks
Increased regulatory scrutiny on AI use in finance
Significant outflows from AI-focused funds

narratives.name: China Chip Self-Sufficiency Push
narratives.thesis: China's efforts to achieve self-sufficiency in chip manufacturing, driven by geopolitical tensions and US sanctions, create both opportunities for domestic players and competitive pressure for international firms.
watchFor: US expands export controls to more categories of chips or equipment
China announces major breakthroughs in advanced chip manufacturing
Significant decline in Chinese demand for foreign chips

narratives.name: Specialty Retail Headwinds
narratives.thesis: Specialty retail is facing challenges due to inflation impacting consumer discretionary spending and a cautious outlook from analysts.
watchFor: Lululemon reports weak international sales growth
Etsy reports decelerating gross merchandise sales (GMS)
Consumer confidence index drops significantly

narratives.name: Gold Watchlist
narratives.thesis: Gold is benefiting from ongoing geopolitical uncertainty and long-term bullish price predictions from strategists, positioning it as a potential safe-haven asset.
watchFor: De-escalation of U.S.-Iran tensions
Stronger-than-expected economic data leading to risk-on sentiment
Fed signals more hawkish stance on interest rates

narratives.name: Farm & Heavy Construction Machinery Watchlist
narratives.thesis: The heavy construction machinery segment is performing well, but this strength is being offset by a downturn in the agricultural equipment business, creating a mixed outlook.
watchFor: Further deterioration in agricultural commodity prices
Significant slowdown in global construction activity
Deere reports continued weakness in farm equipment sales

narratives.name: Banks - Diversified Watchlist
narratives.thesis: Diversified banks are navigating a complex environment of persistent high yields and increasing scrutiny over valuations and analyst ratings, with some facing mixed signals from the market.
watchFor: Net interest margins (NIM) compress unexpectedly
Loan growth decelerates significantly
Increased regulatory pressure or fines

narratives.name: Entertainment Content Expansion
narratives.thesis: Entertainment companies are investing heavily in content expansion and new distribution models, but face challenges in subscriber growth and profitability.
watchFor: Netflix reports subscriber losses or significant ARPU decline
Disney+ fails to meet subscriber growth targets
Increased competition from new streaming services

narratives.name: Discount Stores Watchlist
narratives.thesis: Discount stores are navigating a challenging environment where inflation impacts consumer spending, leading to mixed signals despite some earnings beats.
watchFor: Walmart reports weaker-than-expected sales guidance
Consumer discretionary spending data shows continued weakness
Increased competition from online retailers

narratives.name: Other Industrial Metals & Mining Watchlist
narratives.thesis: The rare earth elements market is influenced by geopolitical tensions and China's dominance, creating a complex and uncertain outlook for miners.
watchFor: New rare earth discoveries outside of China
Relaxation of trade tensions between US and China
Technological breakthroughs reducing reliance on rare earths

narratives.name: Silver Watchlist
narratives.thesis: Silver is facing headwinds after a significant price decline, with analysts debating its future prospects amidst broader market volatility.
watchFor: Industrial demand for silver significantly declines
Further strengthening of the US dollar
Global economic slowdown impacting commodity prices

narratives.thesis: No single narrative is driving Software Infrastructure on the current tape — these names are in scope and watched for a catalyst to break out.

Technology.thesis: The AI infrastructure buildout continues to be the dominant driver for the technology sector, with strong demand for semiconductors, networking equipment, and related software. Despite some competitive pressures and valuation concerns, the underlying demand for AI compute and data center expansion remains robust.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
China expands export controls to advanced AI chips or equipment — significant supply chain disruption
Core CPI print > 0.4% MoM for two consecutive months — forces hawkish Fed repricing, hurting growth stock multiples

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: Consumer cyclical sectors are facing mixed signals. While some areas like residential construction show strength, broader consumer spending is under pressure from inflation, leading to headwinds for retail and restaurant segments. Geopolitical events also add uncertainty.
watchFor: Core CPI print > 0.4% MoM for two consecutive months — further erodes consumer purchasing power
Retail sales data shows significant deceleration — signals broader consumer weakness
Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% — indicates weakening labor market and consumer confidence

Communication Services.thesis: The Communication Services sector is mixed. While digital advertising and content platforms continue to see growth, traditional entertainment faces content expansion costs and valuation scrutiny. AI integration is a key theme across the sector.
watchFor: Major advertising agencies report significant slowdown in ad spending
Netflix reports subscriber losses or significant ARPU decline
Regulatory actions targeting large tech platforms (e.g., antitrust)

Industrials.thesis: The Industrials sector is experiencing mixed trends. Aerospace & Defense benefits from strong backlogs and geopolitical tensions, while Integrated Freight & Logistics sees tailwinds from potential strategic acquisitions. Farm & Heavy Construction Machinery faces a split market with construction strength masking agricultural weakness.
watchFor: Significant cuts to defense spending budgets
Global trade tensions escalate, impacting logistics volumes
Interest rates rise sharply, dampening construction activity

Healthcare.thesis: The Healthcare sector is largely bullish, driven by the continued excitement around GLP-1 obesity drugs and stable performance in healthcare plans. Innovation in medical devices and consumer health products also contributes positively.
watchFor: New clinical trial data for GLP-1 drugs disappoints — cracks the growth thesis
Increased regulatory scrutiny or pricing pressure on pharmaceutical companies
Healthcare plans report significant increases in medical loss ratios

Financials.thesis: The Financials sector is mixed, with some banks facing scrutiny over valuations and high yields, while credit services and asset management show resilience and strategic AI adoption. Regulatory changes and interest rate expectations remain key drivers.
watchFor: 10Y Treasury yield breaks above 5% — increases funding costs for banks, impacts duration-sensitive assets
Commercial real estate (CRE) loan defaults surge — hits bank balance sheets
Fed signals prolonged higher-for-longer rate environment — impacts loan growth and spreads

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The Consumer Defensive sector is largely neutral, with discount stores facing inflationary pressures and household & personal products seeing limited specific news. Consumer resilience and inflation trends are key factors.
watchFor: Core CPI print > 0.4% MoM for two consecutive months — further erodes consumer purchasing power
Consumer confidence index drops significantly — impacts discretionary spending
Walmart or Costco report significant margin compression

Utilities.thesis: The Utilities sector is bullish, primarily driven by the increasing power demand from AI data centers. Independent power producers and renewable energy companies are benefiting from long-term power deals and strategic positioning in the energy transition.
watchFor: Hyperscalers reduce or delay data center expansion plans
Regulatory hurdles or delays for new power generation projects
Significant increase in natural gas prices, impacting power generation costs

Basic Materials.thesis: The Basic Materials sector, specifically industrial metals and mining, is neutral. Geopolitical tensions around rare earth elements create both opportunities and risks, while broader market sentiment remains cautious.
watchFor: Escalation of trade wars impacting raw material supply chains
Significant slowdown in global industrial production
China's economic growth decelerates sharply

Precious Metals.thesis: Precious metals are mixed. Gold is seeing some bullish sentiment from geopolitical uncertainty and long-term price predictions, while silver faces headwinds after a significant price decline. Inflation data and central bank actions remain key drivers.
watchFor: CPI print below 0.2% MoM — reduces inflation hedge demand for gold
Dollar strength continues — weighs on precious metals
Geopolitical tensions de-escalate significantly — reduces safe-haven demand
